title: Smoky Middle-Eastern Eggs Frittata
description: A smoky, juicy, and nostalgic Middle-Eastern dish featuring eggs and fermented labneh cheese.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Add the chopped scallions and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 2 minutes.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, labneh cheese, lemon juice, smoked paprika, salt, black pepper, red pepper flakes, and crushed dried mint.
Pour the egg mixture over the garlic and scallion mixture in the skillet.
Sprinkle the chopped fresh parsley over the top of the egg mixture.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the eggs are set and the frittata is golden brown.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Use a spatula to carefully loosen the edges of the frittata from the skillet.
Slide the frittata onto a plate and sprinkle with crushed red pepper.
Serve hot, garnished with additional chopped parsley and scallions if desired.
Cover and refrigerate any leftovers for up to 3 days.
Reheat the frittata in the microwave or in the oven before serving.
Serve at room temperature or reheat before serving, if preferred, and enjoy!
Use leftover frittata for breakfast or brunch, serve with a side of toast or hash browns.
